Skip to contents
rmdpartials 0.6.3
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Rmarkdown partial example

Ruben Arslan

2019-07-12

Source: vignettes/rmdpartials.Rmd
rmdpartials.Rmd

Introduction

For example, you can make enlarge-able plots:

Graphical relationship 

library(rmdpartials)
library(ggplot2)
x <- rnorm(100)
y <- x + rnorm(100) + 0.5 * x^2
curve <- ggplot(mapping = aes(x, y)) + geom_point()
enlarge_plot(curve, large_plot = curve + theme_classic(base_size = 20), plot_name = "myplot")

Regression 

reg <- lm(y ~ x)
regression_diagnostics(reg)
x
(Intercept) 0.3592409
x 1.0755503

Values vs. fitted values.

Diagnostics (click to show)

Write a partial on the fly 

x <- 5
y <- 9
partial(text = "`r x` `r y`")

5 9

Debug

Learn about environments in partials.

knit_child_debug()
Debug

Working directory

## [1] "/Users/rubenarslan/research/rmdpartials/vignettes"

needs_preview()

## [1] FALSE

is_interactive()

## [1] FALSE

## [1] "/Users/rubenarslan/research/rmdpartials/vignettes"

Viewer null

## [1] TRUE

## [1] TRUE

## NULL

TESTTHAT_interactive

## [1] ""

TESTTHAT

## [1] ""

interactive

## [1] FALSE

## $try.outFile
## A connection with                    
## description ""      
## class       "file"  
## mode        "w+b"   
## text        "binary"
## opened      "opened"
## can read    "yes"   
## can write   "yes"

## [1] 1e-05

## $ts.S.compat
## [1] FALSE

## [1] "/usr/bin/unzip"
